prediction.attackType: T1110 - Brute Force, T1078 - Valid Accounts
prediction.summary: Multiple SSH login attempts targeting privileged accounts were observed. Specifically, the 'admin' account was subjected to a brute-force attack from IP 192.168.1.50, resulting in a successful login. There were also failed attempts against 'admin' from 192.168.1.51 and 'root' from 10.0.0.99, indicating a broader scanning or attack attempt.

timeline.event: Failed SSH password for user 'admin' from 192.168.1.50
Failed SSH password for user 'admin' from 192.168.1.50
Failed SSH password for user 'admin' from 192.168.1.50
Failed SSH password for user 'admin' from 192.168.1.51
Failed SSH password for user 'admin' from 192.168.1.51
Failed SSH password for user 'root' from 10.0.0.99
Failed SSH password for user 'root' from 10.0.0.99
Failed SSH password for user 'admin' from 192.168.1.50
Accepted SSH password for user 'admin' from 192.168.1.50

recommendations: Immediately isolate or block the source IP 192.168.1.50 at the firewall or security group level to prevent further access.
Disable the 'admin' account and force a password reset, ensuring a strong, unique password. Review if MFA was bypassed or not in use.
Investigate the 'admin' session (PID 10255) for any post-compromise activity, command execution, or lateral movement. Correlate with EDR logs if available.
Review authentication logs for other successful logins from suspicious IPs or for other privileged accounts during the same timeframe.
Implement or strengthen rate-limiting mechanisms (e.g., fail2ban) on SSH to prevent future brute-force attacks.
Review the failed attempts from 192.168.1.51 and 10.0.0.99 for any further indicators of compromise or related activity.
